next_steps: Perform permanent section pathology with flow cytometry and immunohistochemical stains (CD20, CD3, EBER-ISH, GFAP)
Send brain tissue for broad-range 16S/18S ribosomal RNA gene PCR and metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS)
Perform lumbar puncture for CSF cytology, flow cytometry, protein, glucose, and infectious studies including Listeria PCR
Obtain serum and CSF testing for Epstein-Barr virus (EBV) and JC virus
Consult Infectious Disease for optimization of antimicrobial coverage tailored to immunosuppressed state (e.g., mycophenolate mofetil use)
Re-evaluate immunosuppression management in the setting of suspected occult central nervous system infection
Perform comprehensive immunohistochemical and flow cytometric analysis on brain biopsy tissue
Submit brain biopsy tissue for metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) and broad-range 16S/18S ribosomal RNA gene PCR
Perform formal microbiological cultures on tissue specifically targeting Nocardia, fungi, and mycobacteria
Evaluate brain MRI with MR spectroscopy and diffusion-weighted imaging to assess for restricted diffusion
Consult Infectious Disease for optimization of antimicrobial coverage considering the patient's chronic immunosuppression
Review systemic imaging and consider bone marrow biopsy if pathology remains ambiguous for lymphoma
Perform neurological reassessment and consider lumbar puncture for CSF studies if clinically stable and intracranial pressure allows
